description:
A vector diagram illustrating the relationship between signal, noise, and phase error in Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) measurements, accompanied by a mathematical condition for phase uncertainty. The diagram displays a green vector labelled "A (signal)" originating from the left and pointing horizontally right. A red vector labelled "n (noise)" originates from the tip of the "A (signal)" vector and points upwards and slightly right, terminating at a red dot. A blue vector represents the sum of the signal and noise vectors, extending from the origin of "A (signal)" to the red dot. The angle between the "A (signal)" vector and the blue resultant vector is labelled 'φ'. A light grey circle is centered at the tip of the "A (signal)" vector, encompassing the red "n (noise)" vector, visually representing the noise contribution.

The accompanying mathematical expression is: "If SNR >> 1 → σ_φ ~ σ_n / µ = D_A". This indicates that if the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) is much greater than 1, then the standard deviation of the phase (σ_φ) is approximately equal to the ratio of the standard deviation of the noise (σ_n) to the mean signal amplitude (µ), with this ratio also being denoted as D_A.
